//: Per-repo Tandem conventions (TEP-6). `acceptanceProbe` is this repo's held-out acceptance-probe recipe (mechanism 5, runnable half): how a *runnable* AC is graded by an INDEPENDENTLY-authored probe instead of the self-graded whole suite. Placeholders: `{spec}` = the SANITIZED Spec id ‚Äî the composite `<tep>/<sp>` with every non-[A-Za-z0-9._-] run replaced by `_`, so spec `6/3` fills as `6_3` (one canonical rule, implemented by fillProbeTemplate; the TEP segment keeps probes from colliding across TEPs) ‚Äî and `{ac}` = the 1-based AC ordinal. The methodology fills these templates; the LANGUAGE lives HERE, so the same methodology works against a Python repo (pytest), a Rust crate (cargo test), Ansible (molecule), etc. `sourcePath` is where the test-author writes the probe; `prepare` (optional) is the BUILD step the closing gate runs ONCE before the per-AC commands ‚Äî needed when `run` targets compiled output, omitted for from-source runners like pytest; `run` is what the gate executes per AC. For this TypeScript extension: sources under src/ compile to out-test/ (tsconfig.test.json include: src/**/*), run by node:test.
//selfVerify: SP-12: the canonical, non-mutating build-and-test command a CODE-author worker runs to self-verify its edits ‚Äî a PEER of `acceptanceProbe`, read by defaultAcceptanceRecipeResolver into AcceptanceRecipe.selfVerify and surfaced into the worker prompt's VERIFICATION BLOCK. It compiles the whole src tree to the GITIGNORED out-test/ (so it never surfaces to the footprint containment check ‚Äî guard-safe by construction) and runs node:test over it. The worker no longer has to reverse-engineer this from tsconfig.test.json or improvise into shared build config.
//testExample: SP-16: a repo-relative path to a CANONICAL example test file ‚Äî a PEER of `acceptanceProbe` / `selfVerify`, read by defaultAcceptanceRecipeResolver into AcceptanceRecipe.testExample (the file's CONTENT) and injected verbatim under the `EXAMPLE TEST` marker into every role:test worker prompt. It shows the repo's fixture-construction + assertion idiom ONCE so a held-out test worker writes its probe straight from prompt + contract instead of independently rediscovering the idiom (reading whole files, hitting the 25k Read cap). Chosen for being right-sized and self-contained: node:test + node:assert/strict, a fixture-stub factory (`fakeQuery`), shared fixture constants (`ACS`), and substring/deep-equal assertions ‚Äî the same idiom a probe reproduces. Declaration-driven and repo-agnostic: a Python/Rust/Ansible repo points this at its own example test; nothing is hardcoded in the orchestrator.
